skill.name: figma-to-tailwind
skill.description: Converts Figma MCP design variables into standard Tailwind CSS classes for frontend implementation.
skill.summary: Turn Figma variables from MCP output into maintainable Tailwind CSS classes.
skill.author: Crearize

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backtick patterns, weak cryptography indicators, and entropy concerns in SKILL.md. Review found Markdown inline code, fenced TypeScript/CSS examples, Japanese prose, and Figma MCP references, with no executable scripts, shell invocation, weak cryptographic use, obfuscation, prompt injection, or data exfiltration evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The external command detections are Markdown inline code and fenced examples that show Tailwind, TypeScript, CSS, and file path references. No evidence found of shell execution, Ruby execution, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax appears inside Markdown documentation and code samples. The skill contains no executable script file or instruction that invokes a shell command.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography alerts point to the frontmatter description and a Figma MCP tool name. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Figma MCP usage and do not contain cryptographic APIs or algorithms. The alert appears to be a substring or heuristic match.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Entropy Heuristic on Markdown Content
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The entropy warning applies to a plain Markdown guide that includes Japanese text and code examples. No evidence found of binary content, encoded payloads, encrypted blobs, or obfuscated instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is human-readable Markdown throughout. Higher entropy is explainable by multilingual text and dense code examples rather than obfuscation.

content.user_title: Convert Figma Variables to Tailwind Classes
content.value_statement: Figma MCP output often contains raw design variables that make frontend code harder to maintain. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to replace those variables with standard Tailwind CSS classes.
actual_capabilities: Maps Figma spacing variables to Tailwind spacing utilities such as padding, gap, and size classes.
Converts width, height, and border radius variables into standard Tailwind class names.
Replaces typography inline styles with configured text utility classes when matching values exist.
Guides color conversion by comparing Figma HEX values with existing shadcn/ui theme variables.
Explains when to add missing values to tailwind.config.ts and figma-tailwind-map.ts.
Provides a checklist for reviewing converted frontend code after Figma MCP generation.
limitations: It is a documentation-only skill and does not run an automated conversion script.
It assumes the project has the referenced Tailwind mapping files and custom text classes.
Color mapping still requires comparing actual HEX values before choosing a class.
It does not validate the final UI visually or replace lint and build checks.

use_cases.title: Clean Figma MCP Output
use_cases.description: Convert generated React markup from raw Figma variables into readable Tailwind classes before committing code.

use_cases.title: Standardize Design Token Usage
use_cases.description: Align spacing, radius, typography, and color implementation with project Tailwind and shadcn/ui conventions.

use_cases.title: Review Styling Consistency
use_cases.description: Identify direct Figma variable usage and request consistent Tailwind class replacements during review.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Basic Spacing
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner cleanup after Figma MCP code generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component and replace Figma spacing variables with standard Tailwind CSS spacing classes where the mapping is clear.

prompt_templates.title: Normalize Layout Classes
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate conversion for size and radius tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert Figma width, height, size, gap, padding, and border radius variables in this component to Tailwind classes. Preserve behavior and explain any unmapped values.

prompt_templates.title: Replace Typography Styles
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced cleanup of generated inline font styles
prompt_templates.prompt: Find typography inline styles from Figma MCP output and replace them with configured Tailwind text classes. Compare the font size, weight, and line height before changing each element.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Full Design Token Usage
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced project review across multiple components
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these components for direct Figma variable usage. Convert safe matches to Tailwind classes, list values that need config additions, and note any color values that require HEX comparison.

output_examples.input: A generated header uses raw Figma spacing, height, radius, and typography variables.
output_examples.output: The header is rewritten with Tailwind spacing, height, radius, and text classes. Unmapped colors are called out for HEX comparison.

output_examples.input: A component contains direct Figma variables in class names and inline style attributes.
output_examples.output: The result removes direct variable usage where mappings exist and lists remaining values that need Tailwind configuration updates.

output_examples.input: A pull request includes Figma MCP generated layout code.
output_examples.output: The review highlights variable-based styling, recommends standard Tailwind replacements, and reminds the author to run lint and build checks.

best_practices: Compare actual Figma values with existing Tailwind and shadcn/ui tokens before choosing a replacement.
Use standard Tailwind classes when values match instead of adding project-specific utilities.
Run lint, build, and visual review after converting generated styling.
anti_patterns: Do not keep raw Figma variables in Tailwind arbitrary value classes when a standard class exists.
Do not create custom utility classes in global CSS for values that belong in Tailwind configuration.
Do not map colors by similar names without checking the actual HEX values.

faq.question: Does this skill call the Figma API directly?
faq.answer: No. It is intended for use after Figma MCP has already provided design context or generated code.

faq.question: Can it convert every Figma variable automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides rules and examples, but unmapped values require manual review and configuration updates.

faq.question: Which frontend stack does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Tailwind CSS projects, especially React projects that use shadcn/ui theme conventions.

faq.question: How should colors be handled?
faq.answer: Compare the Figma HEX value with existing theme values. Use an existing class only when the values match.

faq.question: What files does the guide reference?
faq.answer: It references a Figma to Tailwind mapping file, Tailwind configuration, and frontend coding rules in the target project.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation examples only and no executable command, network, credential, or prompt injection behavior.
